title: The Deep Where Every Thread Forgets Its Colour

features.name: unweaving-loom
features.desc-on-touch: threads pull themselves apart on a loom that has no weaver—each strand loses the memory of what colour it held, what pattern it was meant to make, until all threads are the same faded grey, indistinguishable from the dark around them

features.name: painted-landscape-dissolving
features.desc-on-touch: the terrain around you is rendered in flat painted bands—terracotta earth meeting green sky—but as you watch, the colours blur toward one another, the boundary between them becoming less certain, until you cannot tell where one stops and the other has become

features.name: knot-dissolving
features.desc-on-touch: a knot that once held everything together is coming loose, its structure unravelling not violently but gently, as if it is remembering that it was never truly bound—each loop that releases carries with it the colour it was holding back

features.name: chromatic-forgetting
features.desc-on-touch: where threads have passed through this place, they leave behind not darkness but greyness—the loss of chromatic memory, the way pigment fades from cloth when it is very old, very stored, very forgotten

inscription: colour is just a knot that forgot what bound it; when the knot unties, the colour goes with it

file.name: Weaving_Pattern_Loss_Study.txt
file.note: documentation of thread-colour degradation in storage conditions—pigments fade in predictable order, red last, blue first, until all threads read as the same grey

exits.label: back up toward the knots that still remember their colour
deeper to where colour becomes meaningless
toward a place where threads are still learning to bind
